tomato___late_blight.causes: Tomato blight (also called late blight because it occurs later in the season than “early blight”) is caused by the oomycete phytophthora infestans, which have fungus-like qualities
tomato___late_blight.treatment: You can also apply neem oil or garlic spray as a fungicide
tomato___late_blight.prevention: In the fall, remember to: you can help prevent this disease in future years by practicing proper crop rotation

rice___sheath_blight.causes: In any case, the pathogen does not produce aerially dispersed spores; its dispersal is essentially dependent on hyphae running on plant tissues and producing new infections sheath blight has symptoms similar to stem rot and stemborer infestation
rice___sheath_blight.treatment: The main management options available to minimize sheath blight include:  irri super bag   irri rice quality assessment kit   rice doctor   crop manager   leaf color chart   irri moisture meter   drying systems   harvesting systems   drying basics   harvesting costs   drying strategies   the solar bubble dryer   see more videos on the irri rkb youtube channel links media contact us you do not have access to www
rice___sheath_blight.prevention: High seeding rate or close plant spacing, dense canopy, disease in the soil, sclerotia or infection bodies floating on the water, and growing of high yielding improved varieties also favor disease development

tomato___septoria_spot.causes: Org overview septoria leaf spot is caused by a fungus, septoria lycopersici
tomato___septoria_spot.treatment: Depending on your treatment method, you may have a delay between spraying and harvest
tomato___septoria_spot.prevention: But prevention is an important secondary measure, as it stops it from happening at all

mango___anthracnose.causes: In many regions, pre-harvest infections from organisms like fungi, viruses, bacteria, and nematodes have led to low mango harvests (chowdhury and rahim, 2009)
mango___anthracnose.treatment: None of the accessions tested was resistant, and commercial cultivars generally cannot deliver appropriate qualitative and quantitative yields under humid environmental conditions without regularly applying fungicide sprays (arauz, 2000)
mango___anthracnose.prevention: All mangoes grown for commercial purposes are susceptible, and a resistant cultivar for all strains is not presently available on the market

apple___scab.causes: Apple scab is caused by the fungus venturia inaequalis
apple___scab.treatment: Effective control of apple scab involves applying fungicides and implementing cultural practices like proper sanitation and pruning
apple___scab.prevention: If you’re interested in gaining a deeper understanding of apple scab, its prevention, and treatment, read on

Apple Scab.description: Plant diseases humid-area gardeners suffer with plant disease more often than their arid-area counterparts, as high humidity encourages the kind of fungal and bacterial growth that leads to numerous plant diseases
Apple Scab.cause: Photo by peggychoucair from pixabay table of contents scab is a common issue affecting apple trees, caused by the fungus venturia inaequalis
Apple Scab.remedy: Effective control of apple scab involves applying fungicides and implementing cultural practices like proper sanitation and pruning
Apple Scab.prevention: Planting resistant cultivars is a great method to prevent apple scab in your garden or orchard

Grape Black Rot.description: There is black rot grape treatment, and, with some effort, you can defeat this merciless fungal disease
Grape Black Rot.cause: Black rot of grape (forma parthenocissi) on boston ivy (parthenocissus tricuspidata) black rot of grape; note, hardened, black mummified fruit typical black, scabby spots on green, unripe grapes (vitis) caused by black rot of grapes (arrested development) black, scabby spots on green, unripe grapes (vitis) and black, shriveled, mummified fruit close-up of spots on boston ivy (parthenocissus tricuspidata) caused by black rot of grape overview black rot, caused by the fungus guignardia bidwellii, is a serious disease of cultivated and wild grapes
Grape Black Rot.remedy: If leaves appear the following spring with new lesions, remove these immediately and start a spray treatment program with one of the above-listed fungicides
Grape Black Rot.prevention: Many gardeners would consider this year's crop a lost cause and work toward preventing a recurrence of the disease

Tomato Late Blight.description: Fungal diseases tend to affect older leaves first, so prune them from the bottom up as your tomato plants grow
Tomato Late Blight.cause: Tomato blight (also called late blight because it occurs later in the season than “early blight”) is caused by the oomycete phytophthora infestans, which have fungus-like qualities
Tomato Late Blight.remedy: You can also apply neem oil or garlic spray as a fungicide
Tomato Late Blight.prevention: In the fall, remember to: you can help prevent this disease in future years by practicing proper crop rotation

Potato Late Blight.description: Microbe notes home » agricultural microbiology late blight is a serious fungal disease of plants belonging to the family solanaceae, especially potatoes and tomatoes
Potato Late Blight.cause: This fungus-like pathogen attacks foliage, as well as tubers and fruits, causing them to rot and making them inedible
Potato Late Blight.remedy: Use of fungicides- the control of the blight is accomplished by applying fungicides before infection which are applied in various ways- sanitation- destruction or proper disposal of potato tuber from pits and storehouses is one of the practical methods to reduce the incidence of the disease
Potato Late Blight.prevention: Add cultural practices: proper spacing, no overhead watering, sanitation, rotation

Rice Healthy.description: Symptoms brown spot disease, caused by bipolaris oryzae, is characterized by the appearance of brown, circular spots on the leaves, which can coalesce under severe infection, reducing the photosynthetic area of the plant
Rice Healthy.cause: However, rice production is severely affected by a wide range of diseases caused by fungi, bacteria, viruses, and nematodes
Rice Healthy.remedy: Management to effectively reduce the incidence of false smut and protect both grain yield and quality, it is essential to integrate several management strategies, beginning by avoiding excessive nitrogen application to prevent dense and lush growth that favors fungal development, and at the booting stage, applying a fungicide such as propiconazole 25% ec at 1 ml per liter of water to ensure thorough coverage of the panicles and suppress infection, while overall integrating clean seeds, balanced fertilization, and this timely fungicide application provides the most effective control
Rice Healthy.prevention: Management effective management of rice blast involves an integrated approach that combines the use of resistant varieties such as ir64 and swarna types, alongside balanced nitrogen application to prevent excessive vegetative growth, maintaining good field sanitation by removing infected stubbles and weeds, and ensuring proper crop spacing for aeration
